mcqs.question: What is the main advantage of a linked list over an array?
mcqs.explanation: The main advantage of linked lists is dynamic sizing (can grow/shrink at runtime) and efficient insertion/deletion (O(1) if position is known).

mcqs.question: A node in a singly linked list contains:

options.text: Data and a pointer to the next node
Data and pointers to next and previous nodes

mcqs.explanation: A singly linked list node contains a data field and a pointer (link) to the next node in the sequence.

mcqs.question: What is a self-referential structure?

options.text: A structure that references another structure of a different type
A structure that contains a pointer to a structure of the same type
A structure that cannot be modified
A structure stored in contiguous memory

mcqs.explanation: A self-referential structure contains a pointer member that points to a structure of the same type, forming the basis for linked lists.

mcqs.question: The time complexity of inserting an element at the beginning of a singly linked list is:
mcqs.explanation: Inserting at the beginning of a singly linked list is O(1) because you only need to create a new node and update the head pointer.

mcqs.question: Which type of linked list has the last node pointing back to the first node?
mcqs.explanation: In a circular linked list, the last node's next pointer points back to the first node, forming a circular chain.

mcqs.question: What is the disadvantage of a linked list compared to an array?

options.text: No random access - requires sequential traversal

mcqs.explanation: Linked lists do not support random access. To access the nth element, you must traverse from the head through n-1 nodes.

mcqs.question: The NULL pointer in a linked list signifies:

options.text: The end of the linked list
The node is at the beginning

mcqs.explanation: A NULL pointer in the next field of a node indicates the end of the linked list, meaning there is no next node.
